{"credits": [{"type": "reporter", "user": {"avatar_url": "https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/72152138?v=4", "events_url": "https://api.github.com/users/breakingsystems/events{/privacy}", "followers_url": "https://api.github.com/users/breakingsystems/followers", "following_url": "https://api.github.com/users/breakingsystems/following{/other_user}", "gists_url": "https://api.github.com/users/breakingsystems/gists{/gist_id}", "gravatar_id": "", "html_url": "https://github.com/breakingsystems", "id": 72152138, "login": "breakingsystems", "node_id": "MDQ6VXNlcjcyMTUyMTM4", "organizations_url": "https://api.github.com/users/breakingsystems/orgs", "received_events_url": "https://api.github.com/users/breakingsystems/received_events", "repos_url": "https://api.github.com/users/breakingsystems/repos", "site_admin": false, "starred_url": "https://api.github.com/users/breakingsystems/starred{/owner}{/repo}", "subscriptions_url": "https://api.github.com/users/breakingsystems/subscriptions", "type": "User", "url": "https://api.github.com/users/breakingsystems", "user_view_type": "public"}}], "cve_id": null, "cvss": {"score": null, "vector_string": null}, "cvss_severities": {"cvss_v3": {"score": 0.0, "vector_string": null}, "cvss_v4": {"score": 7.1, "vector_string": "CVSS:4.0/AV:N/AC:L/AT:N/PR:L/UI:N/VC:N/VI:N/VA:H/SC:N/SI:N/SA:N"}}, "cwes": [{"cwe_id": "CWE-1321", "name": "Improperly Controlled Modification of Object Prototype Attributes ('Prototype Pollution')"}], "description": "## Impact

The Edit Fields (Set) node assigned output fields through a dot-notation path setter without restricting the field name, so an authenticated user could name a field after an inherited built-in method path and corrupt a shared global in the main Node.js process. Because that global was used on the request-authentication path, the instance then failed every authenticated request, causing a instance-wide denial of service for all users until the process was restarted.

## Patches

The issue has been fixed in n8n versions 1.123.67, 2.31.5, and 2.32.1. Users should upgrade to one of these versions or later to remediate the vulnerability.

## Workarounds

If upgrading is not immediately possible, administrators should consider the following temporary mitigations:
- Restrict n8n instance access to fully trusted users only.
- Disable or restrict workflow creation and execution permissions for untrusted users.
- Monitor for unexpected process-wide HTTP 500 errors and restart the process promptly if they occur.

These workarounds do not fully remediate the risk and should only be used as short-term mitigation measures.", "ghsa_id": "GHSA-xwx6-jjhv-84p8", "github_reviewed_at": "2026-07-22T22:12:33Z", "html_url": "https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-xwx6-jjhv-84p8", "identifiers": [{"type": "GHSA", "value": "GHSA-xwx6-jjhv-84p8"}], "nvd_published_at": null, "published_at": "2026-07-22T22:12:33Z", "references": ["https://github.com/n8n-io/n8n/security/advisories/GHSA-xwx6-jjhv-84p8", "https://github.com/n8n-io/n8n/commit/f69dfc6dd2178a14ea1624d2e1d403c2e755042f", "https://github.com/n8n-io/n8n/releases/tag/n8n@1.123.67", "https://github.com/n8n-io/n8n/releases/tag/n8n@2.31.5", "https://github.com/n8n-io/n8n/releases/tag/n8n@2.32.1", "https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-xwx6-jjhv-84p8"], "repository_advisory_url": "https://api.github.com/repos/n8n-io/n8n/security-advisories/GHSA-xwx6-jjhv-84p8", "severity": "high", "source_code_location": "https://github.com/n8n-io/n8n", "summary": "n8n: Prototype Pollution via Dot-Notation Field Names Leads To Instance-Wide Denial of Service", "type": "reviewed", "updated_at": "2026-07-22T22:12:34Z", "url": "https://api.github.com/advisories/GHSA-xwx6-jjhv-84p8", "vulnerabilities": [{"first_patched_version": "1.123.67", "package": {"ecosystem": "npm", "name": "n8n"}, "vulnerable_functions": [], "vulnerable_version_range": "< 1.123.67"}, {"first_patched_version": "2.32.1", "package": {"ecosystem": "npm", "name": "n8n"}, "vulnerable_functions": [], "vulnerable_version_range": ">= 2.32.0, < 2.32.1"}, {"first_patched_version": "2.31.5", "package": {"ecosystem": "npm", "name": "n8n"}, "vulnerable_functions": [], "vulnerable_version_range": ">= 2.0.0-rc.0, < 2.31.5"}], "withdrawn_at": null}

title: n8n: Prototype Pollution via Dot-Notation Field Names Leads To Instance-Wide Denial of Service
